add a health check endpoint
❯ add a health check endpoint
⠋ Thinking...⠙ Thinking...⠹ Thinking...
I'll add a health check endpoint to the gateway's HTTP server.

📂 Read internal/gateway/api.go

// ... existing handler code ...

📂 Read internal/gateway/gateway.go

// ... HTTP server setup ...

I can see the HTTP routes are registered in `registerHTTPAPIRoutes`. I'll add a `/health` endpoint.

✏️ Edit internal/gateway/api.go

 // handleHealth returns 200 OK with basic server status.
 func (g *Gateway) handleHealth(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {
     w.Header().Set("Content-Type", "application/json")
     json.NewEncoder(w).Encode(map[string]any{
         "status":      "ok",
         "agent_count": g.agentManager.Count(),
         "uptime":      time.Since(g.startTime).String(),
     })
 }

✏️ Edit internal/gateway/gateway.go

 mux.HandleFunc("/health", g.handleHealth)

Now let me verify it compiles:

⚡ Bash go build ./...

Build successful. The `/health` endpoint is now available and returns:
- `status`: always "ok" if the server is running
- `agent_count`: number of connected agents
- `uptime`: server uptime as a human-readable string
─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

❯
